scope_note: QA Witt Tower Orbit Recession Null cert [454]. Certified null for cert [453]: Gold futures (GC=F) S-orbit months do NOT concentrate in NBER recession months (k_S_rec=0/2, perm_p=1.000). Positive control: QQQ S-orbit 4/6 = 67% in recession (perm_p=0.0006). Cross-asset pattern: risk assets (GSPC perm_p=0.013, QQQ perm_p=0.0006) show S-orbit recession concentration; safe havens (Gold perm_p=1.000, TLT perm_p=1.000) do not. Orbit staircase (S->C=0, C->S=0) holds for Gold too -- universal property. Gold S-states fall POST-recession (Jun 2020, Apr 2021, after Apr 2020 NBER trough), with positive next-month returns (+9.1%, +7.4%). Gold mean_ret_after_S=+8.2% vs GSPC -1.3% -- direction inverts. QA mapping: same as cert [453]; Gold monthly log-returns rank-normalized to Z/27Z; state pair (b,e); S = both b,e divisible by 9. Theorem NT: log-returns are observer projections; orbit class is QA integer state. CERTIFIED FACTS: (C1) Gold N=255, n_rec=20 PASS. (C2) Gold S->C=0, C->S=0 PASS. (C3) Gold k_S_rec=0, perm_p=1.000 >= 0.20 PASS. (C4) Gold perm_p=1.000 (null) vs GSPC perm_p=0.013 (signal) -- risk-asset specific PASS. (C5) Gold mean_ret_after_S=+0.082 > 0 (opposite direction to GSPC) PASS. (C6) QQQ positive control: 4/6 = 67%, log10_p=-3.29, perm_p=0.0006 PASS. 6 checks PASS. PRIMARY SOURCES: Wall HS (1960) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541; NBER www.nber.org/cycles; cert [443] (fixed-layer Gold null). Structural parents: cert [110], cert [443], cert [453]. Validated 2026-06-18.
